title: Fix inverted name display and add present/leaving time to Attendance System

requirements.summary: Fix the attendance system so that registered person names are displayed in the correct (non-inverted) order in the attendance list UI
acceptanceCriteria: Names in the attendance records list are displayed in the same order they were registered (e.g., 'Aswin Nair' shows as 'Aswin Nair', not 'Nair Aswin')
All existing attendance records show names correctly without any inversion
Newly registered faces display names correctly in all attendance views

file_operations.description: Fix the name display logic in the attendance records list to show names in the correct order as registered. Review how names are stored in face descriptors and how they are rendered in the UI table. Ensure that when displaying attendance records, the name field is shown without any string reversal or reordering. Update any sorting or display logic that may be inverting the name order.

requirements.summary: Add 'Present Time' and 'Leaving Time' fields to the attendance system with UI actions to record both timestamps
acceptanceCriteria: The backend attendance record type includes both a presentTime and a leavingTime field (as timestamps or nullable text)
When a face is recognized and attendance is marked, the presentTime is recorded and stored
There is a UI action (e.g., a 'Mark Leaving' button per record or a leave detection trigger) to record the leavingTime for a recognized person
The attendance records list displays both 'Present Time' and 'Leaving Time' columns for each record
If a person has not yet been marked as leaving, the leaving time column shows a placeholder such as 'Still Present' or '-'
Times are displayed in a human-readable format (e.g., HH:MM:SS or locale string)

file_operations.description: Add UI columns for 'Present Time' and 'Leaving Time' in the attendance records table. Display the presentTime from the backend AttendanceRecord in a human-readable format (e.g., using toLocaleTimeString()). Add a 'Mark Leaving' button for each record that does not yet have a leavingTime. When the button is clicked, call the markLeaving mutation with the record ID. Display the leavingTime if available, or show 'Still Present' or '-' if null. Ensure the UI updates reactively when times are recorded.

Add a mutation hook useMarkLeaving that calls the backend markLeaving(recordId: bigint) method. The mutation should accept a recordId parameter, call actor.markLeaving(recordId), and invalidate the 'attendanceRecords' query key to refresh the list after marking a person as leaving. Include proper error handling and loading states.
